# CheckResult { #pycmdcheck.CheckResult }

```python
CheckResult(name, status, message, details=list(), duration=0.0)
```

Result of a single check.

Contains the outcome of running a check, including the status,
a human-readable message, and optional details.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Name of the check that produced this result (e.g., "metadata").

<code>[**status**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckStatus](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckStatus`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The status of the check (OK, WARNING, ERROR, etc.).

<code>[**message**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable message describing the result.

<code>[**details**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[str](`str`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Optional list of detailed messages providing more context.

<code>[**duration**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Time taken to run the check in seconds.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Create a successful check result:

```python
>>> result = CheckResult(
...     name="metadata",
...     status=CheckStatus.OK,
...     message="Package metadata is valid",
... )
>>> result.status
<CheckStatus.OK: 'ok'>
```

Create a result with details:

```python
>>> result = CheckResult(
...     name="linting",
...     status=CheckStatus.WARNING,
...     message="Found 3 linting issues",
...     details=["Line 10: E501 line too long", "Line 20: W503 line break"],
... )
>>> print(result)
⚠ linting: Found 3 linting issues
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [to_dict](#pycmdcheck.CheckResult.to_dict) | Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization. |

### to_dict { #pycmdcheck.CheckResult.to_dict }

```python
CheckResult.to_dict()
```

Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A dictionary with keys: name, status, message, details, duration.

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The status is converted to its string value.

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> result = CheckResult("test", CheckStatus.OK, "Passed")
>>> result.to_dict()
{'name': 'test', 'status': 'ok', 'message': 'Passed', 'details': [], 'duration': 0.0}
```
